title: calophyllum vergens
intro: Calophyllum vergens, also known as the Tamanu tree, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique oval-shaped leaves and fragrant yellow flowers. The plant's oil, extracted from its seeds, has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The oil has been found to promote wound healing, reduce scarring, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as provide relief from pain and inflammation. Calophyllum vergens oil can be prepared as a topical cream or applied directly to the skin, or used in herbal remedies in the form of capsules or tinctures.
